title: agrimonia pilosa var. occidentalis
intro: Agrimonia pilosa var. occidentalis, also known as Western agrimony or soft agrimony, is a perennial plant species in the family Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating menstrual cramps, due to its flavonoids and phenolic acids. Agrimonia pilosa var. occidentalis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
